job_title: Kỹ Sư Vận Hành Mạng & An Ninh Hệ Thống
location: Số 5 ngõ 82 Duy Tân, Cầu Giấy, Ha Noi
required_skills: Security, Web Application Firewall, Networking, Python, MySQL, Linux
job_description:
Chịu trách nhiệm kỹ thuật vận hành, quy hoạch kiến trúc mạng, triển khai dự án, xử lý sự cố đối với các thiết bị mạng như Switch (chuyển mạch), Firewall (tường lửa), Thiết bị quản lý hành vi người dùng, Router (bộ định tuyến), Switch Layer 3, VPN; đảm bảo duy trì an toàn hệ thống và mạng.
Chịu trách nhiệm triển khai, xử lý sự cố và bảo trì các sản phẩm an ninh như: Bastion Host (máy chủ bật), WAF (Tường lửa ứng dụng Web), Kiểm toán nhật ký (Log Audit), Kiểm toán cơ sở dữ liệu, đảm bảo hoạt động ổn định.
Thực hiện triển khai, cài đặt và bảo trì các sản phẩm tự phát triển của công ty cũng như các sản phẩm liên quan đến nền tảng Cloud.
Cung cấp hỗ trợ kỹ thuật và dịch vụ khách hàng, bao gồm như: giải đáp thắc mắc kỹ thuật, xử lý sự cố hư hỏng, đề xuất giải pháp kỹ thuật, đảm bảo vấn đề của khách hàng được giải quyết kịp thời.
Xây dựng quy trình vận hành mạng và soạn thảo tài liệu kỹ thuật chuẩn hóa như: sổ tay hướng dẫn, phương án kỹ thuật.
Đảm bảo hệ thống nghiệp vụ hoạt động ổn định 24/7, phản hồi nhanh chóng và xử lý các sự cố xảy ra
requirement:
Tốt nghiệp Đại học chuyên ngành CNTT, ATTT, Viễn thông hoặc tương đương.
Có từ 1–5 năm kinh nghiệm trong lĩnh vực vận hành hạ tầng mạng & bảo mật.
Hiểu rõ các giao thức: TCP/IP, VLAN, OSPF, BGP, STP, DHCP, DNS,…
Thành thạo Linux/Windows Server, dịch vụ Web (Apache/Nginx), MySQL/PostgreSQL.
Có kinh nghiệm triển khai & cấu hình thiết bị mạng, tường lửa, WAF, máy chủ bảo mật.
Biết scripting (Shell, Python) & sử dụng thành thạo công cụ tự động hóa.
Cẩn thận, kỹ lưỡng, có khả năng làm việc dưới áp lực và phối hợp đa bên.
Có kỹ năng viết tài liệu kỹ thuật & giao tiếp tốt.
Ưu tiên ứng viên giao tiếp được bằng tiếng Trung / tiếng Anh.

company: Bosch Global Software Technologies Company Limited
location: 364 Cong Hoa street, Tan Binh, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: JavaScript, VueJS, Angular, ReactJS, Leadership, HTML5
job_description:
Company Description
The Bosch Group
is a leading global supplier of technology and services. Since the beginning of 2013, its operations have been divided into four business sectors: Automotive Technology, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology.
The Bosch Group comprises Robert Bosch GmbH and its roughly 360 subsidiaries and regional companies in some 50 countries. If its sales and service partners are included, then Bosch is represented in roughly 150 countries. This worldwide development, manufacturing, and sales network is the foundation for further growth.
Bosch Global Software Technologies Company Limited (BGSV) i
s 100% owned subsidiary of Robert Bosch GmbH - one of the world’s leading global suppliers of technology and services, offering end-to-end Engineering, IT, and Business Solutions.
Starting its operation from 2010 at Etown 2 in HCMC, BGSV is the first software development center of Bosch in Southeast Asia. BGSV nowadays have over 4,000 associates, with a global footprint and presence in the US, Europe, and the Asia Pacific region.
With our unique ability to offer end-to-end solutions that connect sensors, software, and services, we enable businesses to move from the traditional to digital or improve businesses by introducing a digital element in their products and processes.
Job Description
A
Lead Front-End Developer
for a global IT workflow product drives the user experience of complex, data-heavy systems. This role requires balancing hands-on technical excellence with the architectural foresight needed to scale products across international markets.
You will lead the front-end strategy for a global IT workflow automation product, guiding architecture and delivery of intuitive, performant experiences for complex, data-heavy use cases. From drag-and-drop workflow builders and real-time dashboards to configuration wizards, you’ll set the technical direction, raise engineering standards, and ensure the UI is responsive, accessible, and reliable across regions and network conditions.
Key Responsibilities
· Technical strategy and architecture:
Define the front-end architecture (React + TypeScript primary) including state management patterns, choosing frameworks and patterns that ensure scalability and maintainability. Drive API contract discipline with backend and define caching/streaming strategies for real-time data.
· Design System Management:
Own and scale the Bosch Digital Design System and component library, ensuring pixel-perfect consistency across all regions and product modules. You will ensure Bosch Corporate Design is applied correctly.
· Workflow Automation UX:
Design and implement intuitive interfaces for complex logic, such as drag-and-drop workflow builders, real-time data dashboards, and integration of wizards.
· Design system leadership:
Own and evolve the component library and design tokens (theming, dark/HC modes); govern adoption via Storybook, documentation, and CI gates (lint, a11y, visual regression). Ensure consistency and scalability across modules and international variants.
· Global readiness (i18n, a11y, performance):
Ensure high performance across varying network conditions and localized support for multiple languages and regional data standards.
· Engineering excellence and security:
Build a testing strategy and implement CI/CD quality gates, bundle analysis, and static checks.
· Cross-Functional Collaboration:
Partner with UX/UI designers to bridge the gap between high-fidelity designs and technical feasibility, and with backend teams to define robust API contracts.
· Team Mentorship:
Mentor engineers, lead code reviews, establish engineering standards and playbooks, and contribute to hiring and onboarding.
requirement:
Must Have
Education:
Bachelor’s or master's degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field.
Experience:
7+ years of professional front-end experience, including 3+ years leading teams or major initiatives on large-scale web apps.
Core Languages:
Expert in modern JavaScript (ES6+), HTML5, CSS3 (Sass/CSS Modules) and modern layout.
Frameworks:
Extensive experience with React, Angular, or Vue.js, including state management (e.g., Redux, MobX). React proficiency building reusable, stateful components and hooks; strong command of state management.
Design system adoption
across 2+ product modules with documented components and usage guidelines.
Build & Deployment:
Proficiency with Git-based workflows, and CI/CD (GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/Jenkins) with quality gates.
Testing:
Strong background in automated testing frameworks like Jest, Cypress, or Playwright.
Deep understanding of Web Accessibility (WCAG) and SEO best practices.
Localized experiences delivered in multiple languages, including at least one RTL locale.
Security awareness
(OWASP front-end risks, XSS/CSP, secure handling of untrusted content).
Agile Mindset:
Proven track record of delivering in fast-paced, sprint-based global environments.
Global Mindset:
Experience delivering software that accounts for regional cultural nuances and varied internet speeds.
Nice to Have
Workflow Expertise
: Experience building tools for IT operations, project management, or SaaS automation is highly desirable.
Project Management:
Ability to manage timelines, define technical roadmaps, and communicate progress to senior stakeholders.
Design token pipelines (Style Dictionary), Storybook, Chromatic/visual regression testing.
Observability (Sentry, Datadog), feature flagging, A/B testing.
Experience with distributed teams and varying network conditions across regions.
Measurable improvements in Core Web Vitals and interaction latency on at least one high-traffic workflow.

location: Tòa nhà Rivera Park 7/28 Thành Thái, Dien Hong, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: QA QC, JavaScript, Python, AI, Automation Test, Tester
job_description:
We are seeking a visionary Lead QC Engineer with 5–7 years of experience to spearhead our transition into AI-augmented quality engineering. In this role, you will architect an Automation-First ecosystem powered by Generative AI and high-velocity deployment tools. You aren't just managing a test suite; you are leveraging cutting-edge CLI tools like Claude Code and robust frameworks like Antigravity to ensure reliability at the speed of modern development.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Automation & Gen AI Execution (65%)
AI-Native Development: Use Claude Code (CLI) to accelerate script generation, perform real-time code refactoring, and automate the creation of complex test suites directly from the terminal.
High-Velocity Execution: Implement and maintain automation workflows using Antigravity, ensuring tests are lightweight, fast-executing, and resilient across different environments.
Gen AI Execution Strategy: Build "Prompt Libraries" and AI agents to handle the heavy lifting of manual-to-automated script conversion (Playwright/Cypress).
CI/CD Intelligence: Integrate AI-driven insights into GitLab/Jenkins pipelines to predict "flaky" tests and optimize execution paths based on code changes.
Self-Healing Frameworks: Design automation layers that utilize LLMs to self-correct scripts when UI elements or API contracts shift.
Process Innovation & Strategy (20%)
Workflow Engineering: Re-engineer the QC lifecycle by deploying AI agents for defect triaging, log analysis, and automated bug reporting.
Tech Advocacy: Continuously evaluate the intersection of Claude Code, LLMs, and Antigravity to minimize "gravity" (friction) in the release cycle.
Efficiency Metrics: Define and track "AI-Lift" metrics—measuring how AI integration reduces time-to-market and increases test coverage.
Leadership & Mentorship (15%)
The "Automation-Mindset": Coach the team to move beyond manual validation, fostering a culture where AI assistants are the primary driver for efficiency.
Strategic Alignment: Partner with DevOps and Engineering leads to align quality gates with rapid, "Antigravity-speed" deployment cycles.
requirement:
Experience: 5–7 years in QA/QC, with at least 2 years in a Lead capacity spearheading automation initiatives.
Automation Mastery: Expert-level proficiency in Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, or Java. Hands-on experience with Playwright, Cypress, or Selenium.
Advanced Tooling & AI:
Claude Code: Proficiency in using Claude’s CLI for agentic coding, automated debugging, and script generation.
Antigravity: Proven experience leveraging Antigravity for high-efficiency test execution and infrastructure management.
LLM Literacy: Expert at using GPT-4, Claude 3.5/4, and AI-powered IDEs (Cursor/GitHub Copilot).
Technical Stack:
Deep knowledge of API Testing (Postman, RestAssured) and Microservices.
Proficiency in Docker/K8s and cloud-native environments.
Soft Skills: A technical "tinkerer" who can explain complex AI workflows to stakeholders and mentor junior engineers in the art of Prompt Engineering.

job_description:
Lead Software Developers
act as the primary point of contact for our clients, ensuring that teams are successful in their delivery. In this role, you will bring a strategic mindset to projects and spend time collaborating and negotiating with clients to bring a technical vision to life. Having a balance of high-level and tactical mindsets is a must, as you will spend time both with clients and your teammates.
At Thoughtworks, we believe in going above and beyond the standard and are committed to delivering best-in-class solutions that exceed our clients' expectations. Our standard engineering and delivery practices reflect our commitment to quality, and our team is always looking to innovate and improve. Lead Developers guide and coach their teams in their implementation and application.

Job responsibilities
You will own the design and architecture of solutions, making pragmatic technology choices and guiding delivery from start to finish.
You will lead delivery by tracking work, mitigating risks, and resolving issues at both team and client levels.
You will build and nurture high-performing teams by fostering growth, wellbeing, and inclusivity, while coaching and mentoring key people.
You will influence and consult with clients, working within constraints to design scalable solutions and drive business value.
You will champion engineering best practices such as TDD, pair programming, clean code, and design patterns.
You will collaborate with stakeholders and teammates to design concepts, build prototypes, and embed UX best practices into solutions.
You will apply continuous delivery and DevSecOps practices to deliver secure, high-quality software quickly and sustainably.
You will remain hands-on with coding, applying multiple languages, tools, and modern practices - never becoming post-technical.
You will leverage insights from the Thoughtworks Technology Radar to adopt new tools, techniques, and approaches.
You will advocate for a culture of feedback, learning, and inclusivity, helping individuals and teams thrive.
requirement:
Technical skills
You are well-versed in at least one
(Java 11 + Spring Boot)
or more modern languages and frameworks, and are comfortable with object-oriented programming.
You have experience
leading software development teams
using Agile, Lean, and/or Continuous Delivery practices.
You have practical knowledge of
engineering best practices
such as TDD, continuous integration, pairing, and infrastructure automation.
You have a learning mindset and enjoy exploring new approaches such as functional programming, event-driven architecture, or platform engineering.
Bonus points if you have hands-on experience with cloud technologies such as AWS, Azure, Docker, or Kubernetes.
Bonus points if you’re familiar with headless commerce platforms like commercetools.
Professional skills
You have experience influencing others and always advocate for technical excellence while being open to change when needed.
You bridge product and technology by helping to translate business needs into software requirements.
You have the ability to develop and execute a technical vision with a focus on business value.
You will act as a mentor for less experienced peers through both your technical knowledge and ability to inspire a team to deliver extraordinary impact together.
You’re resilient in ambiguous situations and can approach challenges from multiple perspectives.

location: City, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: Java, CI/CD, Kafka, API, Spring Boot, AI
job_description:
Role Summary
ITviec's client is seeking an experienced, highly strategic technology leader to serve as the Lead Solution Architect for a critical, large-scale client program. Acting as the senior-most technical authority across the engagement, you will take full ownership of end-to-end architecture, technical delivery governance, engineering leadership, and executive-level stakeholder management
.
This pivotal position requires strong, hands-on engineering credibility combined with deep architectural leadership to ensure that complex technology ecosystems, distributed delivery teams, and high-level client expectations align into a coherent and scalable delivery model. You will operate as the primary technical advisor to client senior leadership, steering strategic decision-making across system architecture, integration strategies, cloud adoption, security, performance, and regulatory compliance
.

Key Responsibilities
1. Technology Strategy & Architecture Ownership
Own and define the end-to-end technology strategy and solution architecture for the program.
Translate business capabilities into scalable technical architecture across applications, data, integrations, and infrastructure.
Govern architecture consistency across multiple domains, squads, and solution streams.
Validate technical decisions, trade-offs, and implementation approaches to ensure long-term sustainability.
Ensure architecture aligns with enterprise standards, regulatory expectations, and client technology roadmaps.
2. Engineering Leadership & Delivery Governance
Provide leadership and direction to architecture, engineering, and delivery teams across the full software lifecycle.
Act as the final technical escalation point for critical delivery issues, scope changes, risks, and dependencies.
Ensure delivery quality through strong technical governance, design reviews, and architecture compliance.
Own the technical RAID process (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) and mitigation planning.
Ensure all non-functional requirements are addressed (security, performance, scalability, reliability, observability, availability).
3. Client & Executive Stakeholder Management
Serve as the primary technical counterpart to client senior stakeholders, including enterprise architects and technology leadership.
Lead technical workshops, architecture reviews, design sessions, and executive alignment discussions.
Present complex technical concepts clearly to both technical teams and senior non-technical leadership.
Build credibility and trust with stakeholders at all levels, from engineers to executives.
4. Solution Consulting & Requirement Translation
Validate and map business requirements into clear technical solution concepts and designs.
Provide consultative guidance on technology choices and implementation approaches
Review business processes and align them with technical design, ensuring end-to-end feasibility.
Produce and maintain high-quality technical documentation and solution design artifacts.
5. Program-Level Coordination & Cross-Team Alignment
Act as a bridge across product owners, engineering managers, solution architects, platform teams, security teams, and vendors.
Ensure alignment between multiple delivery streams such as core banking, cards, payments, digital channels, integration, and data.
Coordinate architecture and delivery consistency across distributed and offshore teams.
Drive collaboration between internal and client teams to remove blockers and accelerate execution.
6. Modern Engineering & Innovation Leadership
Champion modern engineering practices including microservices and distributed architecture, event-driven systems (Kafka), cloud-native development, CI/CD, DevOps, and automation-first engineering.
Promote security-by-design and compliance-by-design principles across delivery.
Promote AI-assisted engineering practices across the SDLC (code generation, test automation, refactoring, documentation), while ensuring correctness, governance, and security.
requirement:
Leadership & Consulting
10+ years of experience in senior technology leadership roles (CTO, Technical Director, Principal Architect, Solution Architect, Technical Consultant).
Strong experience leading large-scale programs involving multiple engineering squads and complex stakeholder environments.
Proven track record in consultative client-facing leadership, including executive communication and decision facilitation.
Technical Expertise
Strong hands-on background in Java, Spring Boot, and API-driven architecture.
Strong experience with microservices and distributed systems.
Experience with event-driven architecture (Kafka).

Maintmaster
is a Swedish company with a 20-year history of delivering software for managing and  structuring maintenance work across a wide variety of industries, primarily in northern Europe.  Today, we are about 50 people but are aiming to grow a lot in the next few years. We work with state-of-the-art tools, frameworks, methods, and programming languages, not just because they are the latest and greatest but also because it is more fun to be close to the bleeding edge product is deployed to Microsoft Azure and currently serves more than 600 customers and 60000 users in 25 countries, and we want your help to make it even better. We offer a great working environment with a high degree of self-organization and independence as well as close collaboration with our Swedish, German, Irish and English colleagues, from developers, QA engineers and UX designers to product owners, salespersons, and project leads.
More information at ;

Maintmaster
is now looking for a quality assurance engineer with a focus on testing and quality assurance within a modern web app built on .NET, React and Microsoft Azure to work in our beautiful office in HANOI, VIETNAM.

What will you do?
You will work in a newly established team of 5-7 people, collaborating alongside an already fully functional and established team with the common task of developing and delivering what is deemed to be the most important functionality for our customers at any given time.
Together with the team, and other teams, you will decide the best way to test and generally ensure that new features are up to our quality standards.
You will plan, design, and execute tests, either manually or create automated test (supported by your teammates)
Your primary work will be in quality assurance, but work may take you into other areas of work and technology too, such as IoT, CI/CD and Azure Infrastructure if you are interested.
The team is jointly responsible for the delivery, from preparation until roll-out.
The team works closely with all stakeholders such as the product owner, UX designer, architect, management, and other development teams. Close collaboration with these is essential.
You are encouraged from day one to contribute to the product backlog and to the team’s way of working. We would love to hear what we should do differently from you.

location: Tòa nhà Sofic, 10 Đường Mai Chí Thọ, Quận 2, An Khanh, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: Cybersecurity, Cloud Security, Software Architecture, Pentest, DevSecOps
job_description:
REPORT TO:
Group Security Manager (CISO)
JOB PURPOSE:
- The Medior Enterprise Security Architect defines, maintains and operationalizes architecture for security services and security solutions across De Heus. The role translates security policy and principles into practical security design patterns, guardrails and approval criteria that can be applied consistently across regions and business units.
- The role provides the security authority to review, challenge and approve designs produced by Cloud & Infra, Applications, Data & Analytics, OT and other teams from a security perspective, ensuring alignment with global security guardrails, standards and risk decisions. The role does not own or replace the full architecture responsibility of those teams; those teams remain responsible for secure designs within their own domains.
- The architect supports architecture reviews, service catalog development, exception handling and security design decisions, while working closely with a senior architecture counterpart during the transition period.

ACCOUNTABILITIES:
1.
Security Architecture Definition & Maintenance
- Define and maintain architecture for security services and security solutions across De Heus.
- Develop practical security design patterns, standards interpretations and guardrails for regional and local execution.
- Document security architecture decisions, reusable patterns and design guidance in a clear and usable way.

2. Security Design Review & Approval
- Review, challenge and approve designs submitted by Cloud & Infra, Applications, Data & Analytics, OT and other teams from a security perspective.
- Provide structured security feedback to improve alignment of peer-team designs with global security standards and guardrails.
- Prepare security architecture review materials and support Architecture Review Board decisions and follow-up actions.

3. Security Service Catalog & Exception Governance
- Contribute to the security service catalog and help define clear business, functional and technical ownership.
- Support governance of exceptions, compensating controls and remediation commitments.
- Help define and maintain security architecture KPIs and inputs for the central security dashboard.

4. Cross-Team Collaboration & Field Enablement
- Contribute to the security service catalog and help define clear business, functional and technical ownership.
- Support governance of exceptions, compensating controls and remediation commitments.
- Help define and maintain security architecture KPIs and inputs for the central security dashboard.

5. Cross-Team Collaboration & Field Enablement
- Work with Regional Security Delivery Specialists to enable knowledge transfer and practical security implementation guidance for business units.
- Build understanding of De Heus platforms, organization, priorities and delivery model to apply security guidance in a practical and business-relevant way.
- Align with the Enterprise Architect, CIO leadership and global IT functions on shared platforms and governance.

6. Professional Growth & Transition Support
- Learn actively from a senior architecture counterpart during the transition period.
- Build deeper knowledge of De Heus security standards, architecture governance, business priorities and platform landscape.
- Continuously strengthen knowledge in security architecture, secure design principles and stakeholder management.

RESULT:
- A coherent set of security architecture patterns, standards and guardrails is in place and adopted across projects and regions.
- Security review inputs and approval decisions are timely, clear and well documented.
- Designs from peer teams show measurable improvement in alignment with global security standards and guardrails.
- The security service catalog is structured and maintained with clear ownership.
- Exception governance is structured and supports measurable reduction of long-term risk.
- Business units and regional teams receive usable security guidance that supports implementation and handover.
- The role shows measurable growth in independence, quality of review and confidence in design decisions during the transition period.
requirement:
Qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cyber Security or a related field. Equivalent experience may be considered.
- Certifications such as CISSP, CISM, CCSP, TOGAF, SABSA or relevant vendor certifications are an advantage.

Experience:
- Around 3 to 5 years of relevant experience in security architecture, security engineering, security solution design or closely related technical roles across IT and Cloud. Experience with OT is an advantage.
- Experience translating security policies or standards into practical technical guardrails, patterns or implementation guidance.
- Experience reviewing technical designs from a security perspective in collaboration with infrastructure, application, data, analytics and engineering teams.
- Working knowledge of identity and access management, segmentation, secure remote access and privileged access concepts.
- Familiarity with exception handling, documentation and security governance processes.
- Experience within De Heus IT or in a closely related De Heus technology role is a strong advantage, because knowledge of the organization, stakeholders, platforms and ways of working can partly compensate for fewer years in a formal security architecture role.
- Experience supporting implementation teams with practical security guidance and knowledge transfer is an advantage.

Competencies:
- Able to translate security policy and principles into practical and implementable security guidance.
- Strong security review mindset and ability to assess peer-team designs objectively.
- Structured and clear in documentation, architecture artefacts and service ownership thinking.

location: 2nd floor, 58 To Huu street, Ha Noi
required_skills: iOS, Objective C, Swift
job_description:
Phát triển SDK & camera pipeline
Thiết kế, xây dựng và duy trì iOS SDK tích hợp AVFoundation/camera stack, edge AI model (CoreML, Vision framework), và API backend để thực hiện luồng chụp hình, phân tích ảnh và xác thực kết quả theo thời gian thực.

Tối ưu kích thước & hiệu năng SDK
Phân tích và giảm thiểu binary size, memory footprint theo từng workflow tích hợp. Áp dụng lazy loading, modular linking, dead-code elimination và các kỹ thuật liên quan để SDK vừa gọn vừa hiệu quả trên nhiều môi trường tích hợp khác nhau.

Bảo vệ toàn vẹn dữ liệu
Thiết kế cơ chế đảm bảo tính toàn vẹn và xác thực của dữ liệu gửi lên từ SDK: payload signing, certificate pinning, chống replay attack và phát hiện dữ liệu bị can thiệp trong quá trình truyền tải.

Bảo mật & chống tấn công ngược
Nghiên cứu và triển khai các giải pháp chống reverse engineering, code injection và API hooking: jailbreak detection, anti-debugging, symbol obfuscation, integrity check runtime. Theo dõi các vector tấn công mới và cập nhật biện pháp phòng thủ phù hợp.

Phân tích logging & crashlytics
Xây dựng hệ thống logging có cấu trúc, tích hợp và khai thác Crashlytics / crash reporting tools để chẩn đoán lỗi production, theo dõi stability metrics và ưu tiên xử lý các sự cố ảnh hưởng đến người dùng.
requirement:

Có kinh nghiệm xây dựng hoặc maintain iOS SDK / framework phân phối cho bên thứ ba
Hiểu rõ iOS memory model, threading, app lifecycle ở mức thấp
Có kinh nghiệm làm việc với REST API, xử lý authentication và bảo mật truyền tải
Quen thuộc với Crashlytics, Firebase, hoặc các công cụ crash/log tương đương
Đọc hiểu và phân tích được crash log, symbolicated stack trace

job_description:

You will be joining a high-energy, global team with a clear vision: make a thriving business within reach of anyone.

and delivering
13+ billion emails/year
. But we aren’t stopping there. We are expanding beyond email into a whole suite of marketing tool.
We are looking for Backend Engineers who are problem solvers. Whether you are a strong builder (Mid-level) or a system architect (Senior), we want you to help us build high-scale infrastructure and new AI products from the ground up.
What You Will Do (The Mission)
You will not just "code tasks." You will drive business impact by working on:
High-Scale Systems:
Build and operate backend services that handle
13 billion+ annual events
. You will solve difficult technical challenges like speed, data processing, and stability.
Innovation & AI:
Explore, utilize, and release the robust backend for an AI application to build a DIFM (Do it for me) marketing tool.
APIs & Integrations:
Design strong internal and public APIs. You will build integrations with partners like Shopify, Zapier, and WooCommerce.
Full Ownership:
You own your code from idea to release. You will work directly with Product Managers and Designers to decide
how
features should work.
requirement:
We hire for
mindset
first, technology second. To succeed here, you need:
Engineering Skills
Our Tech Stack:
We primarily use
Golang and Node.js
on the backend and
PostgreSQL
for our database. But we are open to any background (Java, Python, PHP, etc.)—we care about your logic, foundation, and problem-solving skills more than the specific language.
Critical Thinking:
You don't just fix bugs; you find the root cause. You proactively think about edge cases, database bottlenecks, and security
before
you write code.
System Design (For Seniors):
You understand how to design complex systems (Distributed systems, Databases) and can explain your choices clearly. You can design systems that stay fast even when traffic increases by 10x.
Product Mindset
User Focus:
You understand that code is a tool to solve user problems. You care about the
quality of the product
, not just the quality of the code.
Curiosity:
You ask "Why?" Why are we building this? Is there a simpler way? How does this help the business?
Collaboration

Teamwork:

location: Indochina Riverside, 74 Bach Dang, Hai Chau, Da Nang
required_skills: ERP, SQL, CRM, English, JavaScript, NodeJS
job_description:
About the Opportunity
We are looking for an elite
Internal Tools Builder
to act as the engine behind our company's operations. As we evolve into a full-funnel marketing platform serving over 600k+ verified users, our internal operations, ERP, and tooling need to scale just as fast.
In this role, you will work
directly with company leadership
to identify operational bottlenecks and build custom internal systems from the ground up. You will not be handed a perfect ticket; you will be handed a business problem. We need a highly autonomous Fullstack Engineer with a relentless "builder mindset" who can lead the topic, architect the system, and collaborate with other engineers to get it across the finish line.

What You Will Do
You will not just "write code." You will own internal products end-to-end. Your core challenges will include:
Build the Engine:
Architect and build robust internal tools, ERP modules, and operational systems across the entire stack (Database -> API -> UI) to make our internal teams wildly efficient.
Direct Collaboration with Leadership:
You will work directly with leadership/the boss. You must be comfortable hopping on a call, translating a vague business problem into a technical roadmap, and executing it.
Lead the Topic:
You are the technical owner. You will drive the architecture, make the hard technical trade-offs, and push back when necessary to ensure we are building the right thing.
Mentorship & Collaboration:
You won't be doing it entirely alone. You will pair with and mentor a junior engineer—delegating tasks effectively, doing code reviews, and helping them level up their full-stack skills.
Performance & Architecture:
Balance speed with stability. Know when an internal tool just needs a quick MVP to save the team 10 hours a week, and when a system needs to be highly scalable to handle massive data throughput.
requirement:
What You Need to Be Successful

We hire for mindset first, technology second.

1. The Builder Mindset
Idea to Reality:
You thrive on ambiguity. You can take a vague request ("We are losing track of customer billing edge-cases") and independently architect the database, build the API, and craft the UI to solve it.
Internal Systems Expertise:
You have experience building internal tools, ERPs, CRMs, or admin dashboards. You know how to stitch together third-party APIs, webhooks, and internal databases.
2. Engineering Skills
Deep Experience (5+ Years):
You have a strong foundation in building complex, production-ready web applications.
Fullstack Fluency:
You are completely comfortable jumping between backend logic (API design, complex SQL/Database modeling) and the frontend (React, state management) without skipping a beat.
3. Communication & Leadership
Exceptional Communicator:
Because you work directly with leadership, your written and spoken English must be excellent. You can explain complex technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders clearly and confidently.
Force Multiplier:
You have the patience and communication skills to mentor a junior engineer, breaking down complex architectures into actionable tasks for them.

job_description:
An Internal Developer Platform (IDP) Engineer designs, builds, and maintains an Internal Developer Platform (IDP) to provide developers with a self-service environment that abstracts technical complexity and streamlines software delivery. They implement tools and automated workflows for tasks like infrastructure management, CI/CD pipelines, and environment provisioning, enabling development teams to focus on innovation by reducing their cognitive load and providing access to necessary resources.

Roles, Responsibilities & Typical Activities
Build and maintain the IDP: Design, implement, and manage the set of curated tools, services, and infrastructure that make up the IDP.
Automate workflows: Develop automated processes for common development and operational tasks, such as provisioning cloud resources, deploying code, and managing environments.
Enhance developer experience: Focus on user-friendliness and efficiency to reduce the cognitive load on developers, allowing them to self-service their needs without deep infrastructure knowledge.
Ensure scalability and reliability: Create a platform that is reliable, scalable, and capable of handling evolving application needs over time.
Promote self-service: Provide a centralized and unified interface, often through an internal developer portal, where developers can access the platform's capabilities and resources.
Uphold security and compliance: Integrate robust security measures and governance to protect data and meet regulatory requirements.
Monitor and support the platform: Provide updates, monitor the platform's infrastructure, and offer support to development teams using the IDP.

job_description:
Milient
is a leading SaaS provider specializing in project management solutions, offering tools for timekeeping, resource planning and quality assurance to businesses across the Nordic region. With offices in Norway and Sweden, we are proud of being the Nordic region’s largest SaaS provider within project management, including timekeeping, resource planning, quality assurance, staffing, and recruitment.
Milient
is often used by architects, the staffing industry, advertising, engineering and IT. Milient aims to empower businesses by simplifying complex project management tasks, thereby enhancing productivity and project outcomes. We emphasizes the importance of its employees, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages professional development.

Read more about us:

We are hiring three Software Developers to join a growing product team based in our Hanoi office. You will work within a collaborative team that includes a Tech Lead and consultants based in Poland, contributing to both the maintenance and modernization of existing systems, as well as the development of new features on a MERN stack hosted in AWS.
While the role is full stack in nature, frontend support is available within the broader organisation, so a stronger backend or full stack profile is equally welcome.
A key workstream for this team is the identification and extraction of functional domains within the existing application, decomposing it into standalone, reusable modules that will underpin a broader product portfolio. This is a rare opportunity to combine legacy modernization with genuine greenfield architecture work.

Responsibilities:
Design, develop, and maintain features across the full stack primarily using MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js.
Contribute to the gradual migration of legacy frontend code (jQuery and related technologies) toward modern frameworks and best practices.
Play an active role in identifying and decomposing functional domains within the existing application into standalone, reusable modules – combining legacy modernization with greenfield architecture work to support a growing product portfolio.
Collaborate closely with the Tech Lead, Polish-based consultants, and cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality software.
Participate in code reviews, providing and receiving constructive feedback to uphold coding standards.
Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code with appropriate test coverage.
Identify and address technical debt as part of ongoing development work.
Support deployment and monitoring of applications in AWS environments.
Contribute to Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, stand-ups, and retrospectives.
requirement:

Understanding of software testing principles and experience writing unit or integration tests.

location: The Sentry, 15 Đ. Lê Thánh Tôn, Bến Nghé, Quận 1, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: Automation Test, Cucumber, Android, iOS, CircleCI, Java
job_description:
Our mission is to become the global standard for addressing. Street addresses weren’t designed for 2026. They aren’t accurate enough to specify building entrances, and they don’t exist for parks, rural areas and many parts of the world. This makes it hard to find places and causes problems and inefficiencies on a global scale.
That’s why we created what3words. We divided the world into 3m squares and gave each square a unique combination of three words. It’s the easiest way to find and share precise locations.
Over the last year, what3words has been used in 193 countries, and our monthly active users continue to grow at an impressive pace. Our tech is used by emergency services, delivery companies, eCommerce businesses, ride-hailing apps and NGOs, and is integrated into the navigation systems of millions of cars around the world.
The role:
You’ll be joining our fast-growing tech team, focusing on mobile applications for iOS, Android mobile, and integrated in-car systems. You’ll be responsible for designing and implementing robust, scalable automation in Java using Appium to ensure our mobile users have a seamless, high-performance experience.
Your rigorous attention to detail and excellent communication skills will be essential. If you are passionate about quality and have a proven competence in automating test cases for mobile applications, we’d like to hear from you.
Responsibilities:
Build and maintain mobile test suites in Appium and CI/CD pipelines for our core applications.
Ensure consistent functionality and UI/UX integrity across various device types, OS versions, and screen resolutions.
Conduct a mix of manual exploration and automated regression to find and report mobile-specific bugs, from memory leaks to broken gestures.
Collaborate with Software Engineers and Product Managers to develop effective strategies and test plans for our Mobile Apps.
Find and isolate bugs in backend services and determine their impact on the product or release.
Help troubleshoot issues in production and staging environments.
Conduct post-release/post-implementation testing including regression, performance, and integration testing for backend systems.
requirement:
Essential Skills:
Education:
BSc/BA in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Mobile Automation:
Experience building robust mobile automation frameworks from scratch using open-source tools (e.g.,
Appium, Espresso, or XCUITest
), with a focus on cross-platform functional testing.
Mobile CI/CD Integration:
Experience integrating mobile automated test suites into build pipelines, specifically handling
mobile-specific challenges
like device clouds or emulator/simulator orchestration.
Java Proficiency:
Strong Java programming skills with expertise in
JUnit or TestNG
to drive mobile test scripts.
DevOps Ecosystem:
Experience with build and configuration tools such as
GitHub Actions
and
CircleCI
.
Device & OS Savvy:
A keen eye for mobile-specific nuances, including UI/UX consistency across different screen sizes, OS versions (Android/iOS), and hardware constraints.
Soft Skills:
Excellent communication in spoken and written English, paired with a high-quality "mobile-first" mindset and rigorous attention to detail.
Extra credit if you:
Have worked with Revenue cat or other services that handle in app subscriptions for Apple’s Appstore and Android’s Play store.
Experience working with backend services.
Experience with BDD and implementing Cucumber for automation.
Have worked on native frameworks (XCUITest, Espresso or UIAutomator)
Are able to understand the business context of what you’re doing – understand the value your work creates.
The role is based in our Ho Chi Minh office (at ///become.outlooks.rising). While you can work from the office five days a week if preferred, our Engineering team can choose to work up to two days a week from home. We also offer a six-week remote-work allowance; for up to six weeks a year, you can work from anywhere in the world (as long as you’ve got great WiFi and are happy to work on the HQ timezone).
Diversity & Inclusion
Our mission is to help everyone talk about everywhere, and we believe diverse perspectives make for a better company and better products too. We strongly encourage applications from underrepresented groups and are committed to equality and inclusivity in our hiring processes and company culture.

.

,
the operation team plays a vital role to ensure smooth product experience for our customers

You will be responsible for maintaining our Linux and Windows servers in all environments (production, staging or development). Ensuring the servers are in good working condition (with enough RAM, CPU, etc..).
You will learn to use tools such as Ansible and our CI/CD pipeline to automate our deployment of our services and maintenance of our servers.

You will further improve our CI/CD pipeline and make changes to satisfy the deployment requirements from our dev teams.
You will find out the current issues and optimize our infrastructure and deployment processes.
You will communicate with our customers when needed to resolve issues or escalate the issues to our other development teams.
You will keep track of and report the production, and deployment issues to the management team.
requirement:

Experience with build/continuous integration tools such as Jenkins and GitLab.
Experience with modern build systems such as Gradle.

Experience of managing the full lifecycle of the Build, Test and Release process of web applications.
Experience with AWS or other cloud providers.
Experience with automation tools such as Ansible is a plus.
Strong communication and management skills.
Refer candidates under 30 years old

Participate in database migration projects (e.g., from MySQL to PostgreSQL, or between different versions) and ensure smooth transition with minimal downtime.
Document database architectures, configurations, procedures, and troubleshooting guides; keep technical documentation up-to-date.
Stay up-to-date with the latest features and best practices of PostgreSQL and MySQL, and propose improvements to the database infrastructure.
requirement:
Technical Skills
3+ years of hands-on experience in managing and optimizing PostgreSQL and MySQL databases in a production environment.
Proficient in PostgreSQL and MySQL database design, schema optimization, query tuning, and performance monitoring.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills, able to work effectively with cross-functional teams.
Detail-oriented, responsible, and able to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.

Relevant certifications (e.g., PostgreSQL Certified Professional, MySQL Certified Developer/DBA) are a plus.
Strong English communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with development teams.

required_skills: C++, AI, Embedded
job_description:
We are seeking a Principal Architect to define and own the architecture of our
Unified Edge AI Engine
. In this role, you will
design the software backbone
that orchestrates the entire lifecycle of video analytics on embedded hardware.
You will act as the bridge between Systems Engineering and AI Research. Your mission is to build a modular, graph-based execution engine that balances massive data throughput with limited compute resources, enabling advanced AI models (optimized via quantization/pruning) to run in real-time across diverse hardware platforms.

Scope of Responsibilities/Expectations:
Pipeline Engine Architecture:
Architect a modular, high-throughput Video & AI Pipeline Engine. Design the framework for dynamic graph construction (DAG) where AI models, logic nodes, and image processing blocks can be assembled and executed efficiently.
Heterogeneous Resource Orchestration:
Design the scheduling logic to balance workloads across CPU, GPU, DSP, and NPU. manage critical system bottlenecks such as PCIe Bandwidth, Memory Bandwidth, and Thermal constraints to prevent system stalls.
AI Optimization Strategy:
Define the architectural support for Model Quantization (INT8/FP16) and Network Pruning. Ensure the engine can natively handle optimized model formats, managing the trade-off between inference speed, memory footprint, and accuracy.
Memory & Data Flow Design:
Architect zero-copy data transport mechanisms (DMA, Shared Memory, Ring Buffers) to pass high-resolution video frames and tensor data between pipeline stages without CPU intervention.
Scalability & Abstraction:
Create a hardware-agnostic Abstraction Layer (HAL) that allows the engine to scale from low-power cameras to high-performance AI processors with minimal code changes.
requirement:
System Architecture:
Mastery of Modern C++ (14/17/20)
and architectural patterns for high-concurrency, real-time systems. Deep understanding of Producer-Consumer models, Lock-free queues, and multi-threaded synchronization.
AI Model Optimization: Strong
knowledge of techniques to reduce model complexity
: Quantization (PTQ/QAT), Weight Pruning, and Knowledge Distillation. Experience integrating these optimized models into C++ runtimes (e.g., TensorRT, SNPE/QNN, OpenVINO).
Hardware Intimacy: Deep understanding of SoC architectures (Qualcomm, Ambarella, NVIDIA). Ability to analyze how Cache Coherency, Bus Arbitration, and Memory Controllers impact AI performance.
Profiling & Analysis: Expert ability to use system profilers (e.g., Perf, eBPF, Nsight Systems) to visualize the "Critical Path" of the pipeline and optimize instruction-level performance.
Pipeline Frameworks: Experience designing or heavily customizing media pipelines (similar to GStreamer, MediaPipe, or DeepStream).

Basic Qualifications
Bachelor’s Degree or higher in Embedded AI, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related technical fields.
10+ years of industry experience in Embedded Software Architecture or High-Performance Computing.
Proven track record of designing complex software engines for Video Processing, Computer Vision, or Autonomous Systems.
Experience acting as a System Architect, making critical decisions on resource allocation, memory management, and hardware selection.

location: 39 Nguyễn Duy Hiệu, An Khanh, Ho Chi Minh - Fresher Accepted
required_skills: Product Design, AI, UI-UX, Product Management, English
job_description:
About Flexspace AI
Flexspace AI is the e-commerce + AI revenue platform for coworking — / Shopify / Airbnb for flexible workspace. Seed-funded. We turn operators' websites into high-converting storefronts and run AI agents that price and market their inventory in real time.
What we ship
: mobile-first booking engine that embeds in any operator's site; SmartPricing Agent for dynamic pricing on meeting rooms, day passes, offices; SmartMarketing Agent for promo campaigns; operator dashboard for revenue/bookings/attribution; member portal for end customers; two-way integrations with prominent property management systems.
Who buys it
: coworking operators from single-location indies to multi-country brands. Their revenue is the conversion rate of the screens you'll design — CTAs, catalog, transitions, and checkout aren't decoration here; they're the product.
How we work
: small, agile, AI-heavy. On track to profitability this year. Headcount is deliberately small so each person owns real surface area — tight feedback loops, no bureaucracy, no committees. We measure shipped work — funnel lifts, partner themes shipped, UX issues triaged — not hours. No face-time theater.
Co-located hybrid out of Workflow Thao Dien in HCMC for in-person speed. Wider team is async-first, written-first across US, Israel, Turkey, Philippines, and Vietnam. Weekly product/design sync with the CTO/cofounder — no layers between you and the people deciding what to build.

The Role
Product Designer across our three products: Operator Dashboard, eCommerce customer-facing pages, and Member Portal. You report to the CTO (also a cofounder). No design layer above you.

What design means here.
The hard problem isn't decoration — it's information. Operators juggle hundreds of spaces, prices, members, bookings, integrations, and promotions across locations and timezones. Customers comparing meeting rooms decide in seconds. Members need to find what they're entitled to without reading docs. Your job is to sit with these humans — on calls, in onboarding, over recordings — and reconstruct the mental model they're operating under, including the frustrations they've stopped voicing. Then decide what surfaces when, what's grouped, what's hidden until needed. Empathy is the input; information architecture is the output. Visual polish comes last.
On prioritization.
Not all UX work has the same payoff. You'll look at the same problem from multiple angles — funnel data, operator interviews, revenue, churn signals, support tickets — and pick the surface where your hours move the business most. A polish pass on a screen no one reaches is wasted hours; a 2% lift on the booking funnel is the company's quarter. Judgment over completeness.
On UX decisions.
Bigger calls go to the team before execution. Smaller calls — ship, watch the data, iterate. We're data-driven, not opinion-driven. Real authority over the surfaces you cover; expose your reasoning so we can calibrate together.
Design system.
You're inheriting a partial foundation — it exists, it works, the bones aren't perfect. Today's concrete bottleneck: the system isn't documented well enough for Claude to pick the right component. Closing that gap is core to the role. We also need a north star for where this design system and product experience should be in 12–18 months.

What You'll Do
Audit, watch the funnel across all three apps
. Surface obscure UX issues — flows, transitions, empty states, error states, micro-copy.
Prototype UI/UX adjustments constantly
. Most can be driven with AI if you can guide the frontend correctly.
Theme partner storefronts
. Take a partner's brand (logo, fonts, palette, tone) and tune our eCommerce surfaces to feel native — including non-Latin scripts (Hebrew RTL, Polish diacritics, Spanish, English).
Recommend CTA placements on partner marketing sites
— where, what they say, how they look — to maximize the funnel into our checkout.
Pull data, form an opinion, decide
. Revenue, Mixpanel, GA4 / GTM funnel data, operator feedback.
Make the design system AI-legible
. Each pattern — empty state, error, modal, form, badge, toast — named, exemplified, discoverable from a prompt. Codify repeating patterns as reusable components, documented heuristics, or prompt templates.

How You'll Work With AI
Claude as a peer, not a novelty. You'll iterate on structured prompts and build new ones. Engineers coach you and set guardrails so you can ship UI changes through Claude without first becoming a framework expert. Taste, intent, and judgment are the bottleneck now — not syntax.

What Success Looks Like In 6 Months
You own UX critique across Dashboard, eCommerce, and Member Portal.
Partner theming pipeline is documented, repeatable, faster than today.
You've shipped frontend tweaks directly into the codebase — not just handed off specs.
You're driving CTA recommendations on partner websites end-to-end, with measurable funnel impact.
requirement:
Must-have
Vietnamese, based in Ho Chi Minh City
. Hired on a Vietnamese employment contract. No visa sponsorship or relocation funding.
Solid UI/UX fundamentals
: visual hierarchy, IA, accessibility, error prevention, cognitive load. You can name the heuristic you're applying.
Human empathy
. You can watch users, reconstruct the mental model they're operating under — including the frustrations they've stopped voicing — and let that shape design. User-situation-first, not heuristics-first.
Information design
. You can take a dense, multi-dimensional dataset (bookings × spaces × times × prices × members × policies) and decide what to show, what to hide, what to group, how to layer it.
Interactive portfolio
with at least one full product flow we can click through end-to-end. Live, clickable links — Figma, Framer, Notion, or any platform. State, hover, transitions wired up. No static PDFs or screenshot decks.
AI-native, Claude-fluent
. Daily Claude user, sharp prompter. You know where it shines and where it breaks down.
Frontend-curious
. Willing to ship UI changes into the codebase via Claude with engineer coaching and guardrails.
QA-grade attention to detail
. You spot the obscure flow break, the misaligned state, the off-by-one transition.
Writing and reading English
required for async work. Speaking can be conversational — we coach.
Agency
. You spot things, fix things, ship things — don't wait to be told.
Nice-to-have
E-commerce, marketplace, or booking-site exposure.
Conversion / funnel optimization experience.
Multi-language / RTL exposure (Hebrew, Polish, Spanish) — speeds up partner theming work.

location: 268 Trần Hưng Đạo, Phường Chợ Lớn, TP. Hồ Chí Minh, Cho Lon, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: Product Management, Team Management, Business Analysis, Strategy planning, Presale, Project Management
job_description:
DigiFarm là đơn vị kinh doanh chiến lược mũi nhọn về công nghệ nông nghiệp trực thuộc ExcelTech. Chúng tôi tự hào kiến tạo các nền tảng quản lý nông trại thông minh, nhằm thúc đẩy chuyển đổi số toàn diện cho chuỗi giá trị, phục vụ đa dạng đối tượng: từ các tập đoàn chăn nuôi quy mô lớn, các trang trại tầm trung, cho đến những hộ chăn nuôi cá thể.

DigiFarm đang bước vào giai đoạn tăng tốc mở rộng quy mô mạnh mẽ, với mục tiêu nhân đôi đội ngũ trong 18 tháng tới. Chúng tôi đang tìm kiếm một Trưởng phòng Sản phẩm mang tư duy chiến lược, người sở hữu năng lực "kép": vừa có tầm nhìn bao quát để định hình lối đi cho sản phẩm, vừa sẵn sàng xắn tay áo sát cánh cùng đội ngũ để trực tiếp thực thi và giải quyết vấn đề tại từng điểm chạm.

Hơn cả một vị trí quản lý sản phẩm thông thường, đây là vai trò được quy hoạch với lộ trình rõ ràng để tiến tới vị trí Giám đốc Điều hành Đơn vị (BU Manager) tại DigiFarm. Bạn sẽ vận dụng các phương pháp quản trị mục tiêu chuyên sâu (như OKRs) để dẫn dắt đội ngũ, trực tiếp đàm phán với đối tác và tự tay kiến tạo bản sắc văn hóa làm việc cho tổ chức.
Đây là bệ phóng hiếm có để bạn dẫn dắt bước nhảy vọt của một nền tảng công nghệ đã được kiểm chứng – mang trọn vẹn tinh thần khởi nghiệp với sự linh hoạt tối đa, đồng thời được hậu thuẫn vững chắc bởi nguồn lực dồi dào từ một tập đoàn hàng đầu.
requirement:
Chiến lược & Lộ trình Sản phẩm
Làm chủ chiến lược sản phẩm: Chịu trách nhiệm toàn diện về vòng đời sản phẩm của DigiFarm. Xác định rõ định vị, tuyên ngôn giá trị và thiết lập lợi thế cạnh tranh sắc bén cho cả 3 phân khúc: doanh nghiệp lớn, trang trại tầm trung và hộ chăn nuôi.
Quản trị lộ trình: Xây dựng và duy trì lộ trình phát triển sản phẩm trong 12-18 tháng. Ra quyết định ưu tiên một cách hệ thống dựa trên sự cân bằng giữa giá trị kinh doanh, tính khả thi công nghệ và nhu cầu thực tế của thị trường.
Kiến tạo tầm nhìn: Đóng vai trò "nhạc trưởng", dẫn dắt chiến lược Agri-Data Hub giai đoạn 2027-2030, đặt nền móng vững chắc để DigiFarm trở thành nền tảng dữ liệu nông nghiệp số 1 tại Việt Nam.
Khám phá thực địa: Liên tục rời khỏi văn phòng để trực tiếp xuống trang trại, gặp gỡ người nông dân. Thấu hiểu sâu sắc dòng chảy công việc và những "nỗi đau" thực tế nhất thay vì chỉ dựa vào các buổi họp giả định.
Đo lường hiệu quả: Định nghĩa các thước đo thành công cốt lõi cho từng tính năng hoặc sáng kiến. Theo dõi sát sao tỷ lệ chấp nhận sản phẩm, tỷ lệ giữ chân và hiệu quả kinh doanh.
Quản trị Hiệu quả Kinh doanh
Đồng làm chủ bài toán tài chính: Sát cánh cùng Giám đốc Tài chính (CFO) để kiểm soát bức tranh lãi/lỗ của BU DigiFarm. Theo dõi chặt chẽ doanh thu, biên lợi nhuận gộp (Gross Margin), chi phí thu hút khách hàng (CAC) và giá trị vòng đời khách hàng (LTV).
Thiết kế chiến lược giá: Xây dựng mô hình giá nhạy bén, linh hoạt cho 3 phân khúc: Enterprise (Dịch vụ cao cấp, bán theo giá trị), Mid-Market (Các gói tiêu chuẩn hóa) và SME (Tự phục vụ, trả phí định kỳ - Subscription).
Thúc đẩy Thương mại: Phối hợp chặt chẽ với Trưởng bộ phận Kinh doanh để liên tục tối ưu mức độ phù hợp của sản phẩm với thị trường (Product-Market Fit). Trực tiếp tham gia tư vấn tiền bán hàng (pre-sales) để chốt các hợp đồng Enterprise trọng điểm.
Đánh giá & Quyết sách: Đánh giá hiệu quả kinh doanh hàng tuần cùng Giám đốc Vận hành (COO); tổ chức các buổi phân tích chuyên sâu hàng tháng để kịp thời đưa ra quyết định điều chỉnh chiến lược.
Dẫn dắt Đội ngũ & Kiến tạo Văn hóa
Phát triển Đội ngũ: Trực tiếp dẫn dắt, truyền cảm hứng và đào tạo năng lực cho Bộ phận Sản phẩm & Giải pháp.
Lãnh đạo Liên phòng ban: Cùng các Trưởng bộ phận khác (Kinh doanh, DVKH, Kỹ thuật) tạo thành ban lãnh đạo thống nhất. Điều hướng mục tiêu chung, giải quyết xung đột và ra quyết định nâng cấp khi cần thiết.
Tiên phong ứng dụng AI: Huấn luyện đội ngũ về tư duy làm việc tích hợp công nghệ. Đảm bảo toàn bộ nhân sự thành thạo việc sử dụng các công cụ AI để tối ưu hóa hiệu suất làm việc hằng ngày.
Định hình Bản sắc: Nuôi dưỡng và lan tỏa văn hóa cốt lõi của DigiFarm: "Thấu cảm sâu sắc với người nông dân (Empathy) + Chú trọng sự xuất sắc về kỹ thuật (Engineering Excellence) + Ứng dụng AI một cách thực dụng (Pragmatic AI).

Giám sát hàng ngày các chỉ số cốt lõi: DAU/MAU (Active Users), Retention Rate (Tỷ lệ giữ chân), Session Length (Thời lượng sử dụng), và Churn Rate (Tỷ lệ rời bỏ).
Báo cáo định kỳ về hiệu suất của các tính năng mới sau mỗi đợt Release.
Product Analysis

Phân tích phễu người dùng (User Funnel) để tìm ra các điểm rớt (drop-off). Ví dụ: Khách hàng mở app nhưng tại sao lại thoát ra ngay trước màn hình tracking dữ liệu cá nhân? Do UI/UX khó thao tác hay do lỗi server đồng bộ chậm?
Phân tích phản hồi từ người dùng (User feedback/Reviews trên App Store & Google Play) để khoanh vùng các bug hoặc trải nghiệm kém.

Làm việc với Dev Team để ưu tiên xử lý vấn đề ảnh hưởng đến app performance
Phối hợp với Product Manager/Owner để đề xuất cải tiến tính năng.
Hỗ trợ Marketing/ASO theo dõi tỷ lệ chuyển đổi từ lượt tải (Install) sang lượt đăng ký tài khoản (Sign-up).
requirement:

Có tư duy phân tích dữ liệu nhạy bén (Data-driven mindset)

location: 40A Lam Sơn, Tan Son Hoa, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: Product Owner, Stakeholder management, AI, Agile, Product Management, Business Analysis
job_description:
As a Product Owner at our technology firm, you will own internal products end-to-end — from ideation to launch to ongoing operations — serving our internal teams and departments as your users. You will work directly with the BOD and Heads of other departments as your primary stakeholders, leveraging AI tools aggressively across the product workflow, and driving delivery through strong communication, stakeholder expectation management, and self-management. This role suits someone who thinks like an owner, not an executor.

End-to-End Product Ownership:
Own the full lifecycle of internal products — from ideation, through development and launch, to post-launch operations. Define product vision, roadmap, priorities, and success metrics aligned with organizational goals. Take full accountability for outcomes, not just output.
AI-Powered Product Work:
Leverage AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) extensively across the workflow — drafting specs, writing user stories, prototyping, research, documentation, and testing — to multiply your throughput and the team's velocity.
Stakeholder & Expectation Management:
Work directly with the BOD and Heads of other departments as your primary stakeholders. Gather needs, align priorities, communicate trade-offs clearly, and manage expectations proactively. Say "no" constructively when needed and defend decisions with data.
Delivery & Demo:
Partner with the Development team through implementation. Run sprint demos for stakeholders, coordinate releases, handle UAT with internal users, and ensure features ship on time and meet business goals.
Operations & Post-Launch:
Monitor product performance and health after launch. Support internal users across departments, handle operational issues, collect feedback, and iterate. Own the product in production — not just during build.
Self-Management & People Coordination:
Drive your own priorities with minimal supervision. Coordinate across cross-functional teammates without formal authority — influence, unblock, and follow through.
Data-Driven Decisions:
Use usage data and internal user feedback to prioritize backlog, validate hypotheses, and recommend improvements.
Ad-hoc Tasks:
Execute additional assignments as requested by the Line Manager.
requirement:
KNOWLEDGE & EXPERIENCE
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Business Information Systems, or a related field.
Minimum 2-3 years of experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or senior Business Analyst with proven end-to-end product ownership.
Experience working on internal / B2E (business-to-employee) products, enterprise tools, or internal platforms is a strong plus.
Hands-on, daily experience using AI tools to accelerate product work — specs, prototypes, research, documentation, analysis.
Solid understanding of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Agile/Scrum; comfortable operating in AI-assisted development environments.
SKILLS
Strong AI fluency:
able to use AI tools effectively across product tasks — prompt well, validate output, integrate AI into daily workflow.
Fluent in Vietnamese
(clear, concise, and persuasive communication)
and
Professional working proficiency in English.
Stakeholder management with senior leaders:
comfortable working directly with BOD and Heads of other departments — align competing priorities, manage expectations proactively, and communicate with executive-level clarity.
Proficiency in writing user stories, acceptance criteria, and creating workflow diagrams (AI-assisted is fine and encouraged).
Comfortable running demos, UAT sessions, and stakeholder presentations.
SQL and basic data-analysis skills are a plus.
ABILITIES
Strong self-management:
organized, proactive, and drives tasks to completion without constant supervision.
Manages people without authority:
coordinates, influences, and unblocks teammates across functions.
Owner mindset:
takes responsibility from idea to operation — not just hand-off at delivery.
Expectation-setting:
calm under pressure, honest about trade-offs, and able to say no constructively.
Growth-oriented:
embraces new technology (especially AI) and continuously sharpens the craft.
An active listener who can calmly champion and defend new ideas.

Product Delivery & Optimization

ROLE OVERVIEW
We are looking for a  Product Tester who is passionate about building reliable, high-quality digital products — not just executing test cases. You will play a key role in ensuring that every feature delivered by the Product team works smoothly, performs consistently, and creates a seamless experience for families using TeenCare AI.
You will work closely with Product Managers, Designers, and Engineers throughout the product lifecycle — from understanding requirements and designing test scenarios to validating new releases before they reach real users.
If you enjoy finding edge cases, thinking critically about user experience, and improving product quality through structured testing and collaboration, this role is for you.
ABOUT TEENCARE AI
At TeenCare AI, we work at the intersection of education, family, and technology. Each week, teens work with dedicated 1:1 mentors. These sessions generate meaningful behavioral insights that help parents better understand and support their children.
We combine human mentorship with technology to create tools that help families make better decisions in everyday life. Our platform is already supporting families across Southeast Asia, and we are building toward a system that can scale globally.
WHAT YOU’LL DO
Ensure product quality across the platform
Create and maintain detailed test cases, test plans, and testing documentation
Execute functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing across web and mobile platforms
Identify, document, and track bugs clearly with reproducible steps and impact analysis
Validate product requirements and user flows before release
Collaborate closely with the Product team
Work alongside Product Managers and Engineers to understand business logic and feature expectations
Participate in requirement discussions, sprint planning, and product reviews
Provide feedback on usability, edge cases, and product consistency from a user perspective
Support UAT processes and release validation
Improve testing processes and quality standards
Contribute to improving QA workflows, test coverage, and release processes
Help define testing standards and best practices within the team
Support automation testing initiatives where applicable
Monitor product stability and proactively identify potential risks before deployment
Experiment and improve
Explore new testing approaches, QA tools, and automation opportunities
Continuously improve testing efficiency and reporting quality
Stay updated on product quality trends and testing methodologies
TESTING STACK & ENGINEERING EXPECTATIONS
Testing & QA
Manual Testing
Functional Testing
Regression Testing
Integration Testing
Exploratory Testing
User Acceptance Testing (UAT)
Tools & Collaboration
Jira / Trello / ClickUp
Postman
Chrome DevTools
TestRail or similar QA management tools
Technical Knowledge
Basic understanding of APIs and client-server architecture
Familiarity with databases and data validation
Understanding of SDLC and Agile/Scrum workflows
Basic automation testing knowledge is a plus
Collaborative Stack
Node.js
PostgreSQL
MongoDB
Redis
requirement:
Technical
1–2 years of experience in software testing or QA roles (project experience is acceptable for junior candidates)
Strong attention to detail and structured problem-solving skills
Ability to write clear bug reports and testing documentation
Familiarity with web/mobile application testing
Basic understanding of APIs, databases, and product workflows
Mindset
Curious and proactive — you naturally explore edge cases and ask thoughtful questions
Strong ownership — you follow issues through until resolution
Collaborative by default — you communicate clearly with Product and Engineering teams
User-focused — you care deeply about delivering smooth and reliable user experiences
Comfortable working in a fast-moving startup environment with evolving priorities
WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE
Within the first 3 months, you will:
Own testing processes for multiple product features and releases
Build reliable test cases covering core user flows
Detect and report critical issues before they impact users
Improve release quality and reduce production bugs
Contribute meaningfully to a stronger quality culture within the Product team

location: Nam Ky Khoi Nghia, Xuan Hoa, Ho Chi Minh
required_skills: Project Management, Jira, English, Agile, Scrum
job_description:
1. Quản lý dự án end-to-end
Lập kế hoạch tổng thể, tổ chức triển khai và quản lý dự án từ khởi động đến khi Go-live và bàn giao vận hành.
Theo dõi, kiểm soát tiến độ, phạm vi, ngân sách và đảm bảo dự án đạt được mục tiêu chất lượng đề ra.
Xác định tiêu chí Go-live, tổ chức nghiệm thu, bàn giao và đào tạo đội ngũ vận hành.
Chủ động xử lý các vấn đề phát sinh, đề xuất điều chỉnh kế hoạch khi cần thiết để đảm bảo mục tiêu dự án.
2. Quản lý phạm vi và yêu cầu thay đổi
Xác định, thống nhất và kiểm soát phạm vi công việc của dự án.
Quản lý các yêu cầu thay đổi (Change Request), đánh giá tác động về tiến độ, chi phí và chất lượng trước khi trình phê duyệt.
Đảm bảo sự nhất quán giữa phạm vi, mục tiêu và kết quả đầu ra của dự án.
3. Quản lý tiến độ, chất lượng và hiệu quả thực thi
Xây dựng kế hoạch chi tiết, quản lý task/backlog và điều phối nguồn lực phù hợp.
Tổ chức dự án theo mô hình Agile/Scrum (khi áp dụng), phối hợp chặt chẽ với Product Owner trong việc xây dựng roadmap và lộ trình triển khai giải pháp.
Theo dõi, cập nhật và báo cáo tiến độ định kỳ hoặc đột xuất theo yêu cầu của các cấp quản lý.
Kiểm soát chất lượng các deliverables, kịp thời đưa ra biện pháp khắc phục khi phát sinh sai lệch.
4. Quản lý rủi ro và vấn đề
Nhận diện, đánh giá và dự báo các rủi ro tiềm ẩn của dự án.
Xây dựng phương án giảm thiểu, kế hoạch dự phòng và theo dõi việc thực hiện.
Chủ động giải quyết các vấn đề phát sinh; trường hợp vượt thẩm quyền, kịp thời báo cáo và đề xuất phương án lên cấp lãnh đạo cao hơn.
5. Giao tiếp, phối hợp và quản lý các bên liên quan
Đóng vai trò cầu nối giữa các bên liên quan (Nghiệp vụ, CNTT, Đối tác…).
Lập kế hoạch truyền thông, yêu cầu và phân bổ nguồn lực phù hợp cho từng giai đoạn dự án.
Giải quyết xung đột, đảm bảo sự phối hợp nhịp nhàng giữa các đơn vị.
Chuẩn bị và hoàn thiện các hồ sơ, tài liệu dự án theo quy định.
6. Kỹ năng lãnh đạo và phát triển đội ngũ
Gắn kết và tạo động lực cho các thành viên trong team dự án.
Hỗ trợ, định hướng và tháo gỡ khó khăn để các thành viên hoàn thành nhiệm vụ được giao.
Xây dựng môi trường làm việc chủ động, trách nhiệm và hướng đến kết quả.

job_description:
About CoderPush
CoderPush
is a remote-first technology company that partners with startups and global businesses to build scalable, high quality software products. We focus on long-term collaboration, clear communication, and delivering real impact through strong engineering and product thinking.
Please find more at:

About the Role
We are looking for a Senior Project Manager who can operate independently from day one - owning delivery contracts, evaluating and giving feedback on technical resources, and executing end-to-end delivery across complex fintech engagements. This is not a coordination or reporting role. You will be expected to assess engineering estimation, challenge scope, and make commercially sound decisions in a financial services context.

Responsibilities
Own delivery of assigned projects: scope control, schedule, budget, quality, and risk across multiple concurrent engagements
Evaluate technical resources assigned to projects - assess capability, provide structured feedback, and make resourcing recommendations to leadership
Challenge and validate engineering estimations; identify technical risks before they become delivery problems
Define and figure out scope independently in mid-to-late project phases where requirements are ambiguous or evolving
Lead integration work with multiple external vendors (core banking, market data, payment gateways, cloud providers)
Represent client interests when negotiating with vendors (pricing, SLAs, funding programs such as AWS)
Manage subcontractors: selection, onboarding, performance monitoring, and invoices
Run regular client governance (status, escalations, acceptance criteria, UAT) at CTO/PMO level
Produce and maintain project artefacts: plans, RAID logs, integration specs, cutover plans
Ensure compliance with client security and regulatory requirements relevant to banking/securities
What Makes You Successful in This Role
Client-first mindset with strong commercial judgment
Comfortable making decisions in ambiguous situations
Strong prioritization and escalation skills
Pragmatic, negotiation-oriented, and detail-focused
Able to balance relationship management with delivery discipline
Success Metrics in the First 6 Months
Successfully hand over and stabilize delivery for at least 1 large integration project with agreed timeline and quality expectations
Drive vendor negotiations that help reduce client cost or secure additional support, with clear documentation of outcome
Keep scope creep caused by missed requirements to below 5%
Maintain positive client satisfaction results in line with target CSAT
requirement:

At least 5 years project management in IT
Hands-on experience delivering at least 2 software projects for banks or securities firms
Sufficient software development knowledge to evaluate tech team estimations, identify scope gaps, and challenge technical decisions
Working knowledge of financial products and operations - lending, trading, payments, securities - beyond consumer-level familiarity
Proven ability to own and define ambiguous scope in later project phases; not limited to task tracking and progress reporting
Experience evaluating developer and BA profiles, providing structured performance feedback, and making resourcing calls independently
Proven experience managing multi-vendor integrations (APIs, batch feeds, middleware)
Strong vendor negotiation experience with documented commercial outcomes (cost savings, SLA improvements, or secured credits)
Excellent stakeholder management - able to interact confidently and independently with bank CTOs/PMOs
Experience managing subcontractors and remote teams
Structured communicator: executive-level project documentation and status reporting
Vietnamese (native) + business-level English for vendor / partner / client discussions
Expert in Agile and Waterfall; able to design and adapt delivery frameworks per engagement
Nice to have
Certifications: PMP, Prince2, or Certified ScrumMaster
Background in securities trading operations, FIX protocol, or core banking platforms
Prior experience with AWS commercial programs and cloud migration projects
Experience building or working alongside AI/automation development teams
What Makes You Successful in This Role
Client-first mindset with strong commercial judgment
Comfortable making decisions in ambiguous situations
Strong prioritization and escalation skills
Pragmatic, negotiation-oriented, and detail-focused
